extraction.one_line_summary: VALVERDE Alejandro won the uphill sprint finish after GEGENHART Tao's premature attack with 200m remaining, while LUTSENKO secured yellow via time bonuses.
tactical_patterns: Movistar setup revealed: LOPEZ paced final 2km to deliver VALVERDE from fifth wheel, exploiting numerical advantage over isolated GC threats.
LUTSENKO targeting time bonuses rather than stage win shows Astana prioritizing GC over glory with teammate IZAGUIRRE placing second overall.
GEGENHART's attack with 200m remaining failed because solo Ineos rider launched too far out against teams with multiple finishers present.
CRADDOCK's repeated breakaway success (second consecutive day at front) suggests EF deliberately burning domestique before mountains to save climbers.

rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: At 41, closing speed in uphill sprint exceeded younger specialists, suggesting peak form timing differs from standard GC contenders
rider_signals.POSTELBERGER Lukas: negative: Yellow jersey faded on late climbs after minimal teamwork earlier, confirming Bora prioritizing KELDERMAN over jersey defense
rider_signals.GEGENHART Tao: negative: Mistimed solo move with 200m left when outnumbered by Movistar/Astana riders reveals poor tactical awareness in group finishes
rider_signals.LUTSENKO Alexei: positive: Secured yellow via strategic third-place finish for time bonus rather than contesting win, showing mature GC race management
rider_signals.LOPEZ Miguel Angel: positive: Willingness to pace final 2km for VALVERDE rather than own result indicates Movistar flexible hierarchy favoring in-form rider
rider_signals.CRADDOCK Lawson: neutral: Second consecutive day initiating successful break suggests EF using him as expendable asset before decisive mountain stages
